 {"id":"ft-gc4hz","title":"retry: cancelled operations must not count as circuit-breaker failures","description":"with_retry_and_circuit_cx pattern-matches Err(_) and calls circuit.record_failure() unconditionally. Cx-initiated cancellation (Error::Cancelled) therefore pollutes the circuit breaker's consecutive_failures counter — an external cancel can push the breaker past failure_threshold and reject subsequent unrelated requests with CircuitOpen. Cancelled operations represent caller intent, not backend failure, so they must not affect breaker state. Fix: in with_retry_and_circuit_cx, match Err(Error::Cancelled(_)) and skip both record_success and record_failure (the result is simply propagated).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T09:53:13.437155Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:22:33.645671Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:22:33.644374Z","close_reason":"Landed in 503c9506 (retry: exempt Error::Cancelled from circuit-breaker failure count) + 765743d5 (regression test) + 79029eeb (test fix: private fields -> snapshot()).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"ft-gkqej","title":"[STRATEGIC/LOW] Audit-fatigue markers in project memory ('don't re-sweep') age poorly under high-concurrency agent commits — regression blindspot","description":"Modes-of-reasoning F6: MEMORY.md contains 'patterns-subsystem-bug-sweep-2026-04-20 [don't re-sweep]', similar for capture-storage, mux-term-codec. Combined with (a) 200+ recent commits, (b) 50+ agent identities committing concurrently, these 'clean' verdicts age fast. Regressions into already-audited modules escape scrutiny.  {"id":"ft-gofz5","title":"[EPIC] Release engineering: first tagged release, cargo install validation, and binary distribution pipeline","description":"# Goal
Establish a working release pipeline so that \`cargo install --git ... ft\` actually works, release binaries are available for major platforms, and there are tagged versions users can reference.

# Background
The reality check found:
- Only 1 git tag exists ("backup-before-rewrite") — no version releases
- \`cargo install\` is advertised in README Quick Install but never validated
- 6 GitHub Actions workflows exist (ci.yml, release.yml, etc.) but release.yml has never been triggered for a real release
- The project is pre-1.0 with 4,288 commits but zero releases
- Build is resource-intensive (~793k LOC core library) which may cause cargo install to fail on memory-constrained machines

# Why This Matters
A project that can't be installed by its advertised method has zero users. Even for internal/swarm use, tagged releases let agents pin to known-good versions. Release hygiene signals project maturity.

# Architectural Intent
1. Validate that \`cargo install --git ... ft\` succeeds on a clean machine (Linux x86_64, macOS arm64 at minimum).
2. Create v0.1.0 tag with honest release notes reflecting current capabilities.
3. Ensure release.yml workflow produces downloadable binaries.
4. Add a CI job that runs \`cargo install\` from git to catch install regressions.

# Acceptance Criteria
1. \`cargo install --git https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankenterm.git ft\` succeeds on Linux x86_64 and macOS arm64.
2. A v0.1.0 tag exists with release notes that honestly describe working features, partial features, and known limitations.
3. release.yml produces downloadable binaries for at least Linux x86_64 and macOS arm64.
4. CI includes a cargo install smoke test that runs on every push to main.
5. README Quick Install section includes minimum system requirements (RAM, disk, Rust nightly version).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:55:35.783442Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T02:07:18.344985Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T02:07:18.344593Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","distribution","goal-line","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-gofz5","depends_on_id":"ft-d0ez0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:59:00.127254Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-gofz5.1","title":"Validate cargo install from git on clean Linux x86_64 and macOS arm64 environments","description":"# Goal
Prove that the advertised Quick Install command actually works on fresh machines.

# Design
1. On a clean Linux x86_64 environment (CI runner or fresh container):
   - Install Rust nightly (matching rust-toolchain.toml)
   - Run: cargo install --git https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankenterm.git ft
   - Verify: ft --version produces output
   - Verify: ft doctor runs without crash
   - Record: peak RAM usage, disk usage, wall time

2. On macOS arm64 (CI runner or local):
   - Same steps as above
   - Additional: verify LLVM/clang dependency resolution (cc alias issue documented in MEMORY.md)

3. If install fails, diagnose and fix:
   - Likely issues: memory exhaustion (793k LOC), missing system deps, cc alias conflict
   - Fix in Cargo.toml, build.rs, or CI configuration
   - Document minimum requirements

# Acceptance Criteria
1. cargo install succeeds on both platforms with exit code 0.
2. ft --version and ft doctor both work post-install.
3. Peak RAM and disk requirements documented.
4. If build requires CC/CXX overrides, document in README.","notes":"2026-04-07 CyanIvy: install/docs command corrected; targeted docs smoke test passes via rch; clean-host harness now passes and records WezTerm bridge prerequisites honestly; remaining gap is true clean-host cargo-install-from-git validation on Linux/macOS because rch in this environment is classifying cargo install as non-offloaded. Added workflow .github/workflows/cargo-install-validation.yml for that next step.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":120,"created_at":"2026-04-06T18:55:46.599217Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T01:59:46.274240Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T01:59:46.261176Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ci","install","release"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-gofz5.1","depends_on_id":"ft-gofz5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:55:46.599217Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 ## Decision

    - Checkpoints should precede irreversible effects.

 5. `INV-005` Runtime API access is centralized.
-   - Prefer `runtime_compat`/`cx` boundary surfaces over ad hoc mixed runtime calls.
+   - Prefer `runtime_async`/`cx` boundary surfaces over ad hoc mixed runtime calls.
 
 ### Legacy-to-target mapping and user-visible behavior
 
 | Legacy | Target | Required behavior | User-visible implications |
 |---|---|---|---|
 | `tokio::spawn` | scope-owned spawn / `cx::spawn_with_cx` | no orphan tasks | shutdown traces identify owners instead of silent background exits |
 | `tokio::select!` | explicit race/select with cancellation handling | no dropped critical messages | cancellation outcomes are deterministic and reason-coded |
-| `tokio::time::*` | `runtime_compat::*` then Cx-aware adapters | deterministic timeout semantics | timeout failures include stable reason codes + remediation hints |
+| `tokio::time::*` | `runtime_async::*` then Cx-aware adapters | deterministic timeout semantics | timeout failures include stable reason codes + remediation hints |
 | lossy channel/send patterns | reserve/commit or equivalent guarded sequencing | no command/event silent loss | cancellation/loss is explicit in logs and operator messages |
 | ambient runtime startup | unified runtime bootstrap (`CxRuntimeBuilder` policy) | single policy surface per role | startup/shutdown messaging is consistent across CLI/watch/robot/web |
 
 ### Anti-patterns (must reject)
 
 1. New direct `tokio::*` usage in doctrine-migrated modules.
-2. Mixed direct `asupersync::*` + `runtime_compat::*` without explicit boundary rationale.
+2. Mixed direct `asupersync::*` + `runtime_async::*` without explicit boundary rationale.
 3. `Cx::for_testing()` in production paths.
 4. Detached spawn without ownership/cancellation narrative.
 5. Mapping cancellation/panic into generic errors that erase reason visibility.

 ## 1. Doctrine Principles
 
 ### 1.1 Single Runtime Target
 
-asupersync is the sole async runtime for FrankenTerm. tokio remains only as a
-transitional fallback behind `#[cfg(not(feature = "asupersync-runtime"))]`.
-No new code may introduce direct tokio dependencies; all async primitives flow
-through `runtime_compat.rs`.
+asupersync is the sole async runtime for FrankenTerm. No new code may introduce
+direct tokio dependencies; project-owned async primitives flow through
+`runtime_async.rs`.
 
 ### 1.2 Cx Everywhere
 
 Every async function that performs I/O, acquires locks, or sends on channels
 must accept `cx: &mut Cx` (or obtain one from its scope). `Cx::for_testing()`
-is permitted only inside `runtime_compat.rs` shims and `#[cfg(test)]` blocks.
+is permitted only inside `runtime_async.rs` shims and `#[cfg(test)]` blocks.
 Production code must never create ad-hoc Cx instances.
 
 ### 1.3 Structured Concurrency
@@ -92,7 +96,7 @@ All effects are gated through the Cx capability token. This enables:

-1. Introducing new direct `tokio::*` usage in non-`runtime_compat` production modules.
+1. Introducing new direct `tokio::*` usage in non-`runtime_async` production modules.
 2. Spawning detached tasks without explicit scope ownership and shutdown semantics.
 3. Collapsing `Outcome::{Cancelled, Panicked}` into generic errors without preserving reason in logs.
 4. Performing cancellation-sensitive sends/writes without reserve/commit or equivalent guarded sequencing.

 ## Runtime Doctrine (Version 1.0.0 Contract)
@@ -79,22 +87,22 @@ Normative runtime doctrine now lives in:
 2. `INV-002`: Capability context (`Cx`) propagation is explicit; no ambient runtime effects.

 ### Canonical mapping (old -> target)
 
 | Legacy pattern | Migration target | Required behavior | User-visible behavior change |
 |---|---|---|---|
 | `tokio::spawn(...)` | `cx::spawn_with_cx(...)` or scope-owned spawn | no orphan tasks; explicit owner | shutdown progress and stalled-owner diagnostics become explicit instead of silent background exits |
 | `tokio::select!` race patterns | asupersync race/select with explicit cancellation handling | no dropped critical messages | contention paths report deterministic cancellation reasons instead of generic timeout noise |

 ### Anti-patterns (reject in review)
